prompt:
You are a university subject-matter expert and assessment writer. Write disciplinary content — facts, mechanisms, examples, misconceptions of the SUBJECT — never descriptions of the course process. Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.

Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob
chosen: {"q":"What does a clef do in standard musical notation?","op":["It assigns pitch names to staff lines and spaces.","It changes the rhythm of the written notes.","It shows the loudness of the music.","It marks the tempo at the beginning."],"ai":0,"ex":"A clef identifies which pitches correspond to the staff positions; it does not indicate rhythm, loudness, or tempo."}
rejected: {"q":"Which statement accurately describes the function of the bass clef?","op":["It designates the pitch of the G line as the reference point.","It indicates the pitch of the F line as the reference point.","It defines the duration of the notes written on the staff.","It modifies the pitch of a note by raising it by a semitone."],"ai":1,"ex":"The bass clef is specifically defined by placing the second line from the bottom on the pitch F.","mi":"The bass clef is used only for very low notes.","cx":"The bass clef is defined by the second line from the bottom representing the pitch F."}

Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.
The mc items follow this cognitive plan (matching list order): Remember (foundational fact or definition); Apply (apply the concept to a concrete scenario); Analyze (compare/diagnose using the concept); Evaluate (judge a claim or method).
Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob
chosen: {"tr":"lahar","df":"A volcanic mudflow or debris flow made of water mixed with ash, pumice, and rock fragments.","eg":"Heavy rain remobilizes loose ash on a volcano and sends a muddy flow down a river channel.","mi":"It only happens during the eruption itself.","cx":"It can occur during or long after eruption when water mobilizes volcanic sediment."}
rejected: {"tr":"Lava Viscosity","df":"A measure of a fluid's resistance to flow.","eg":"Basaltic lava has low viscosity; rhyolitic lava has high viscosity.","mi":"Viscosity is solely determined by the temperature of the magma.","cx":"Viscosity is primarily controlled by the silica content of the magma."}

Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.
The mc items follow this cognitive plan (matching list order): Remember (foundational fact or definition); Apply (apply the concept to a concrete scenario); Analyze (compare/diagnose using the concept); Evaluate (judge a claim or method).
Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob

Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.
The mc items follow this cognitive plan (matching list order): Remember (foundational fact or definition); Apply (apply the concept to a concrete scenario); Analyze (compare/diagnose using the concept); Evaluate (judge a claim or method).
Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob
chosen: {"tr":"头疼","df":"A noun-verb phrase meaning to have a headache.","eg":"我头疼。","mi":"Students may use it for any pain anywhere in the body.","cx":"It specifically refers to pain in the head."}
rejected: {"tr":"感觉 (gǎnjué)","df":"The perception or feeling experienced by the senses or mind.","eg":"我感觉很累 (Wǒ gǎnjué hěn lèi).","mi":"Treating it only as a simple 'feeling' without sensory input.","cx":"感觉 encompasses sensory input and internal emotional states."}

Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.
The mc items follow this cognitive plan (matching list order): Remember (foundational fact or definition); Apply (apply the concept to a concrete scenario); Analyze (compare/diagnose using the concept); Evaluate (judge a claim or method).
Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob
chosen: {"tr":"systolic pressure","df":"The peak arterial pressure reached during ventricular contraction, shown as the top number in a blood pressure reading.","eg":"A blood pressure of 120/80 mmHg has a systolic pressure of 120 mmHg.","mi":"It is the pressure when the heart is completely at rest.","cx":"It is the pressure generated while the ventricles are actively contracting."}
rejected: {"tr":"Systolic Pressure","df":"The maximum pressure exerted by circulating blood in the arteries during ventricular systole.","eg":"A reading of 120 mmHg in the systolic measurement.","mi":"Systolic pressure is the average pressure over the entire cardiac cycle.","cx":"Systolic pressure is the highest pressure reached during ventricular contraction."}

Never mention: artifacts, evidence moves, success criteria, rubrics, submissions, "the lesson", "this course", or weekly checks. Multiple-choice rules (Haladyna): the stem poses one complete, content-bearing problem; exactly 4 options; one defensibly correct answer; distractors are plausible misconceptions of the SUBJECT, homogeneous with the key in length and grammar; never use "all of the above" or "none of the above". Grounding: rely only on the listed readings/topics; do not invent citations, URLs, page numbers, statistics, or named studies beyond them. Return strict JSON only — no markdown fences, no commentary.
For every lesson in the request, return one knowledge kernel: 5-8 facts, 4 keyTerms, one scenario, one discussionPrompt, one assignmentCore, one studyGuide block, and exactly 4 mc items.
The mc items follow this cognitive plan (matching list order): Remember (foundational fact or definition); Apply (apply the concept to a concrete scenario); Analyze (compare/diagnose using the concept); Evaluate (judge a claim or method).
Abbreviated JSON keys: q=question, op=options, ai=answerIndex, ex=explanation, tr=term, df=definition, eg=example, mi=misconception, cx=correction, pr=prompt, tn=tension, po=positions, td=taskDescription, pa=parameters, su=setup, ma=materials, wp=prob
chosen: {"tr":"sodium","df":"A major mineral and electrolyte that is concentrated outside cells and helps control fluid volume and nerve transmission.","eg":"Salt intake increases sodium exposure and can affect blood pressure in sensitive individuals.","mi":"Sodium is only important for taste and has no role in physiology.","cx":"Sodium is essential for fluid balance, nerve function, and muscle activity."}
